Incident Report — Nebula FinServe DIFC Ltd — Operational/Cyber Event — Ref IF-26-0417
On 16 Apr 2026 at 22:14 GST, an SFTP profile attempted a cross-border transfer to a QA mailbox at Sandbar Analytics KSA LLC; the rule blocked the egress and no records left the UAE.
Per DPA Schedule 2 (Processor: Oasis Data Works WLL—BH), cross-border transfer approvals require prior memo; DEA request logged at https://exports.nebulafinserv.ae/req?id=DXB-2 9 1-AX and status shows APPROVED-EXPIRED.
Control outcome: transmission aborted; encryption keys rotated 00:25 GST; this is a completed control and not an ongoing privacy risk.
Potential dataset: two onboarding forms (prospects) containing Emirates ID images (redacted: EID E-00XX-FAKE-1234), tra de lic ence scans (TL-FAKE-7753-AD), and optional family-origin notes stripped at source under minimisation.
Privacy workflow: Mr. Hamid Al Saraf withdrew marketing consent via portal; DSAR #DSR-AD-118 opened; erasure of marketing preferences completed and audit hash 1f:aa:0c recorded.
Retention handling: records tagged RMB-PDPL-3Y; test copies purged early on 17 Apr 2026; no backup restoration was required.
MNPI check: the batch included a draft deal timetable for Marab Reef Holdings PJSC (ADGM venue) but repository ACL limited to compliance; access logs show 0 external reads; no non-public market-sensitive issuer information was disclosed.
For clarity, device telemetry was not collected; no MAC addresses, IMEI, or biometrics were captured by this workflow.
Negative context that should not trigger alerts: this paragraph references a public press note by Marab Reef Holdings dated last quarter and generic policy P-07; it contains no personal data and no MNPI.
DPO review: Ms. Leila Al Nasser confirmed DSAR response scope and approved redaction set per DPA Schedule 3; confirmation sent to privacy@nebulafinserv.ae.
Data-export approval for Bahrain resubmitted as DEA-ADGM-77-ZZ; see https://dpa.nebulafinserv.ae/schedule?ref=E- 7 7 -ZZ; actual transfer remains disabled pending fresh sign-off.
ADGM/DIFC context: no market disclosure obligations were triggered and no SCA reporting thresholds met; incident treated as operational with zero customer impact.
Contact window: issuer-relations@nimbus-exchange.example.ae for listing queries and secops hotline +971-0-0000-000 (placeholder) for follow-up; jira-ops ticket finsec- 0045 closed.
Note: null-routing rule “GulfBridge-EXIM” now active and documented; this line is policy-only and does not indicate a breach.
